message:
test(ft-e8brh): harden cancel_races_child_creation race coverage

The previous test spawned 8 creator threads through one barrier and
then cancelled. On any host with ≥ 8 cores the creator threads
typically completed `parent.child(...)` before main reached
`parent.cancel(...)`, so every round reduced to the pre-existing-child
case already covered elsewhere. The interesting race — a new child
being created *concurrently with* cancel — was rarely exercised.

Changes:

* Increase creators from 8 to 64 so some are preempted by the OS
  scheduler mid-call regardless of core count.
* After the barrier, each creator calls `thread::yield_now()` and
  every fourth creator sleeps 50µs, giving main's `cancel()` a real
  window to land first.
* Each creator samples `parent.is_cancelled()` immediately before
  calling `child()` and reports the observation back.
* Bump round count from 20 to 64.
* Add a post-run assertion that at least one round observed
  `parent.is_cancelled() == true` before a `child()` call — i.e. the
  cancel-first interleaving *actually* occurred. If that never happens
  the test has silently regressed back to the pre-existing-child case
  and the assertion fails with a specific diagnostic pointing at loom
  / shuttle as the next step.

The core invariant being tested is unchanged: after cancel, every
child (created pre- or post-cancel) must observe cancellation.

Build: compiles under --no-default-features --features mcp,asupersync-runtime.
Runtime verification on the local host was blocked by a macOS dyld
bottleneck pattern (test binary stuck at 32-byte RSS in _dyld_start
under heavy concurrent compile load from other agents) independent
of this change.
